source.description: Six-panel raster contact sheet generated for the June 6, 2026 archive marker-doodle lesson, reviewed before cropping.
source.notes: Prompt required one consistent pool float doodle with no sticker border, badge frame, embedded labels, arrows, signatures, or fake UI.

process_strategy: The sequence builds the donut float ellipse, inner hole, thickness, stripes, water ripples, and marker fill before the final panel only thickens and clarifies existing parts.

final_elements.name: outer float ring
final_elements.description: A big tilted donut-shaped inflatable ring.

final_elements.name: inner hole ellipse
final_elements.description: A centered opening that follows the same tilt as the outer ring.

final_elements.name: ring thickness
final_elements.description: A soft inflated side edge that gives the float volume.

final_elements.name: stripe panels
final_elements.description: Curved stripe sections wrapped around the ring.

final_elements.name: water ripple marks
final_elements.description: Small blue ripple marks around and through the float opening.

final_elements.name: shine gaps
final_elements.description: Small white highlight gaps on the inflated surface.

final_elements.name: bright marker fill
final_elements.description: Aqua, coral, and yellow marker fills on the established ring stripes and water.

frames.visible_job: Draws the outer ring and inner hole ellipses.
Adds the ring thickness and soft side edge around the float.
Adds curved stripe panels around the already-drawn float ring.
Adds water ripple marks and highlight gaps on the existing float.
Adds bright marker fill to the established stripe panels and water ripples.

final_step.visible_job: Thickens the black outline, evens the marker fill, sharpens shine gaps, and clarifies the water ripples already present.

rejection_checks: No ring, hole, thickness, stripe, water ripple, shine, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may ink, fill, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
